title: hotato conversation artifact manifest, schema conversation.v1
description: The canonical Conversation Artifact manifest (Phase-1 design D): binds every piece of evidence for one conversation by sha256, so `hotato.conversation.verify` re-hashes the referenced children and REFUSES on any digest mismatch (the evidence-kernel refuse-not-downgrade posture; a tampered child is refused, never silently accepted). HONESTY INVARIANTS: origin.kind ('real'|'simulated') is REQUIRED so synthetic is never conflated with real (invariant 5), and a simulated origin MUST carry its simulator block; no overall_score anywhere; created_at is supplied by the caller, never Date.now() on a deterministic path.

origin.description: The synthetic/real axis (invariant 5). `kind` is REQUIRED. A 'simulated' origin MUST carry a simulator block (enforced by the if/then below and by validate_conversation_doc).

created_at.description: Supplied by the caller (e.g. the capture receipt's time). Never Date.now() on a deterministic path.

artifacts.description: Evidence children bound by digest. Each entry is optional; the closed set is audio/transcript/trace/timing/assertions.

artifact_ref.description: A child bound by sha256 (the same content-addressing hotato.fleet.store uses). `path` locates the child relative to the conversation dir so `verify` can re-hash it; the sha256 is the binding -- verify refuses on mismatch.
